rulings.Case Title: JAY WANLASS VS. ASBESTOS CORPORATION LIMITED
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 1. Fireman's Fund Insurance Company's motion to set aside entry of default and default judgment is granted. Fireman's Fund is allowed to bring said motion. (See Western Heritage Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (2011) 199 Cal.App.4th 1196.) The default entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on September 21, 2012 and the default judgment entered against defendant Associated Insulation of California on December 8, 2015 are set aside pursuant to the court's inherent, equitable power to set aside a default on the ground of extrinsic fraud or mistake. Relief is not authorized pursuant to C.C.P. § 473(b) because the motion was filed more than six months after both entry of default and default judgment. Plaintiff's request for a continuance is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: WAYNE RUNNESTRAND et al VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication By Defendant Kirkhill-Ta Co
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant Kirkhill-TA Co.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' first, second, third and fourth causes of action are denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to demonstrate that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Runnestrand was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant did not support its motion with evidence that plaintiffs failed to provide meaningful responses to comprehensive interrogatories designed to elicit all the evidence plaintiffs have to support their contention of liability, precluding defendant from sustaining its burden under the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant also failed to sustain its burden of presenting evidence that conclusively negates an element of plaintiffs' causes of action. Mr. Pierce's declaration is insufficient to do so. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: WAYNE RUNNESTRAND et al VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication By Defendant Gencorp Inc.
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 2. Defendant GenCorp Inc.'s motion for summary judgment and alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiffs' first, second, third and fourth causes of action are denied. Plaintiffs concede that defendant sustained its initial burden or, at a minimum, they do not argue that defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. The deposition testimony of Mr. Runnestrand and the declaration of Dr. Cohen create a triable issue whether Mr. Runnestrand's exposure to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant (v-44 and v-52 rubber) was a substantial factor in causing or contributing to his risk of developing lung cancer. Defendant's objections to Dr. Cohen's declaration are overruled. Defendant's request for an evidentiary hearing is denied. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: MARTIN GRANADOS ET AL VS. HONEYWELL INTERNATIONAL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for July 14, 2016, Thursday, in Department 503, Line 4. Defendant T.M Cobb Company's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden under either prong of Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Granados was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Defendant is precluded from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar because: Mr. Granados testified that Cobb supplied fire doors with mineral cores to J&M; defendant admits that plaintiffs identified James Granados as a product identification witness and defendant failed to demonstrate that James does not have knowledge that Mr. Granados was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant; defendant's Separate Statement did not cite to or rely on plaintiffs' special discovery responses in an attempt to argue they are factually devoid on the issue of asbestos content of any doors that defendant supplied to which Mr. Granados was exposed. Defendant also failed to submit affirmative evidence sufficient to negate an essential element of plaintiffs' claims. Plaintiffs' objections to Mr. Cobb's declaration are sustained as to paragraph 3. Plaintiffs' other objections are moot because no other facts from Mr. Cobb's declaration to which plaintiffs object were contained in defendant's Separate Statement. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
